core_principle: Split by cognitive demand: a strong model decides (few calls), a cheap model executes (most calls); calls whose output enters the final product and need judgment go Tier-S, scaffolding/draft/verifiable-proposal calls go Tier-E with an escalation valve.

operation_candidates.name: Role-tier mapping
operation_candidates.trigger: A multi-call LM workflow; need to know which calls are safe to downgrade.
action: For each LM call ask: is it thinking (plan/route/judge/propose) or doing (rewrite/extract/format/apply)?
Thinking -> Tier-S (strongest reasoner); doing -> Tier-E (cheap model).
Note that execution-tier calls dominate call count and therefore cost.
operation_candidates.output: A (call -> tier -> model) mapping table plus expected cost structure.
evidence: output/dspy-sop-skill/SKILL.md (decide which model optimizes vs is task model)

operation_candidates.name: The architect+editor recipe
operation_candidates.trigger: Strong reasoner with weak execution (dirty diff/format), or any reason-then-land step to decouple.
action: Strong model = architect: emit a natural-language plan only, no final format.
Cheap model = editor: turn the plan into a format-correct artifact with a lean prompt and its preferred format.
Aider auto-switches editor to editor-diff / editor-whole.
operation_candidates.output: Two-step pipeline: propose (strong, free text) -> apply (cheap, strict format).
evidence: Polyglot Pass@2: o1-preview 79.7% -> o1-preview+Sonnet 82.7% -> o1-preview+o1-mini 85%; Sonnet self-paired 77.4% -> 80.5%

operation_candidates.name: Fallback-escalation valve
operation_candidates.trigger: Cheap executor fails/degrades on a subset of inputs.
action: Define detectable failure signals: invalid format, failing test, schema validation failure, low self-eval.
On failure -> escalate that step to Tier-S and retry (or retry once with a more robust format first).
Track escalation rate as a health metric.
operation_candidates.output: Execution tier with an escalation valve; escalation-rate metric.
evidence: https://aider.chat/docs/troubleshooting/edit-errors.html (escalate model / change format on edit failure)

operation_candidates.name: Format-by-tier
operation_candidates.trigger: Choosing the output format for a Tier-E call.
action: Give the cheap model the parse-safest format (whole file / simple schema), not byte-sensitive diffs.
Never wrap code or complex artifacts in a JSON tool-call.
evidence: https://aider.chat/2024/08/14/code-in-json.html (all models, incl. Sonnet, do worse wrapped in JSON)

operation_candidates.name: Cheap-optimizer / expensive-task (the mirror)
operation_candidates.trigger: You auto-tune prompts / search / orchestrate (a meta layer) while the served task call is expensive.
action: Run the meta layer (optimize/propose/search) on a cheap model.
Reserve the expensive model for the task call that enters the final product.
Criterion stays: quality-into-product -> strong; scaffolding -> cheap (this is the mirror of OP-1/2).
evidence: https://github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy/issues/1596 (gpt-4o-mini optimizer + gpt-4o task: parity, lower cost)

operation_candidates.name: Distill-after-split
operation_candidates.trigger: Split is stable; want to compress the execution tier into a cheaper local small model.
action: Use the strong model / optimized pipeline as teacher; distill a student small model for Tier-E.
Recompile/retest when the task-LM family changes; large-model verbose CoT demos make small models parrot length without reasoning.
operation_candidates.output: Fine-tuned small executor + strong decision model combo.
evidence: output/dspy-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case B (GPT-4 pipeline breaks on Llama-3-8B)

operation_candidates.name: Supervisor/worker tiering
operation_candidates.trigger: Multi-agent orchestration: a routing/coordinating agent plus several executing sub-agents.
action: Workers (execute a concrete subtask) -> cheap model.
Budget for the supervisor's translation token overhead in the total cost.
evidence: output/langgraph-sop-skill/SKILL.md Case 2 (supervisor uses more tokens than swarm due to translation)

dilemma_cases.title: Is Aider architect mode worth the cost?
dilemma_cases.rule: Gain = (quality delta) x (task value) - (extra call cost). Reasoner already clean at editing -> delta ~= 0, don't split. Reasoner editing dirty (o1/o3) -> delta large (79.7%->82.7%->85%), split.

dilemma_cases.title: When does a cheap executor degrade enough to escalate?
dilemma_cases.rule: Add detectable failure signals; escalate failing steps only (not the whole tier). Track escalation rate: <10% healthy; 10-30% watch; >30% the step belongs in Tier-S, reclassify. Never probabilistically escalate everything (= back to single tier).

anti_patterns: Splitting without measuring quality+cost delta on your own task.
Loading the cheap executor with the strong model's format burden (byte-sensitive diff, code-in-JSON).
Splitting with no escalation valve (long-tail failures reach the product).
Treating the split point as a binary switch instead of a tunable slider.
Not recalibrating after swapping the execution-tier model family.
